text: The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to open the outer cortex of the left ovary, carefully pulling the instrument anteriorly to avoid puncturing the dermoid cyst. A long incision is made over the anti-meso-ovarian border, allowing for separation without bleeding.

The surgeon uses a Maryland dissector and a grasper to carefully perform cystectomy on the dermoid cyst located on the left ovary. The Maryland's jaws are opened to create a proper dissection plane, ensuring the ovary remains intact during enucleation. The cyst is successfully separated without rupture, maintaining the integrity of the surrounding tissue.

The surgeon carefully manipulates the cyst using atraumatic graspers to avoid rupture, alternating between holding the cyst and stripping the ovary. The cyst is nearly enucleated, with only a few remaining attachments being gently separated. The ovary is then examined for bleeding or residual dermoid tissue, and it appears intact.

The surgeon introduces a 10 mm cannula and an endobag through the laparoscopic ports. The intact dermoid cyst is placed inside the endobag, which is then carefully pulled through the cannula. An assistant holds the endobag while the surgeon uses ovum forceps to rupture the cyst inside the endobag, ensuring the bag remains intact during the process.
Thank you very much for watching this video of the laparoscopic management of dermarcyst. Have a nice day. Thank you very much.
